Name: Bellsprout
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

Name: Weepinbell
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Burned and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

Name: Victreebel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Once ingested into this Pokémon’s body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

Name: Cacnea
Flavor-Text: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Packs of them follow travelers through the desert until the travelers can no longer move.

Name: Kricketune V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand instead. You can’t use more than 1 Exciting Stage Ability each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cherubi
Flavor-Text: The deeper a Cherubi’s red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

Name: Cherrim
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).
Flavor-Text: The faint scent that emanates from its full blossom entices bug Pokémon to it.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Durant
Effects: For each of your Durant in play, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

Name: Scatterbug
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The beaks of birds can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Heal all damage from 2 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

Name: Fomantis
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It loves the sunshine. When it basks in the sunlight regularly, it grows up with vivid coloration.

Name: Lurantis
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it slashes into its enemies, it looks like it’s dancing. Its elegant appearance has led some to call it the most glamorous Grass Pokémon.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, it’s violent enough to crush anyone it sees as an enemy.

Name: Blipbug
Flavor-Text: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

Name: Flapple V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C][C] more.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flapple VMAX
Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Entei
Effects: You may attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Victini V
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Victini VMAX
Effects: Attach up to 3 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Tepig
Flavor-Text: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.

Name: Pignite
Flavor-Text: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

Name: Emboar
Ability-Effect: Your Single Strike Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

Name: Salandit
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 90 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.

Name: Sizzlipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

Name: Centiskorch
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent flips a coin for their Burned Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup, it doesn’t recover from that Special Condition even if the result is heads.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

Name: Horsea
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Seadra
Flavor-Text: It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

Name: Kingdra
Ability-Effect: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move any amount of [W] Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It’ll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime
Effects: Discard any number of Item cards that have the word “Ball” in their name from your hand. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

Name: Remoraid
Flavor-Text: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine’s underside to scavenge for leftovers.

Name: Octillery
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Corphish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.

Name: Empoleon V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Basic Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Frillish
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.
Flavor-Text: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

Name: Jellicent
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each [W] Energy card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

Name: Bruxish
Flavor-Text: Its skin is thick enough to fend off Mareanie’s spikes. With its robust teeth, Bruxish crunches up the spikes and eats them.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Haymaker.
Flavor-Text: While it’s often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.

Name: Electivire
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Lightning Slam.
Flavor-Text: A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Tapu Koko V
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Koko VMAX
Effects: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, their Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Yamper
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Poké Ball card, a Great Ball card, or 1 of each from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Boltund
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack can be used for [L].
Flavor-Text: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

Name: Galarian Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because Galarian Slowpoke eat the seeds of a plant that grows only in Galar, their tails have developed a spicy flavor.

Name: Spoink
Flavor-Text: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

Name: Grumpig
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Attach [P] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like until your Pokémon and your opponent’s Pokémon have the same total amount of Energy attached.
Flavor-Text: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

Name: Espurr
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There’s enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can’t be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.

Name: Meowstic
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It’s when they’re protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.

Name: Mimikyu V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Necrozma V
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dottler
Effects: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

Name: Orbeetle
Effects: For each Energy attached to this Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon, except Orbeetle, and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what’s around it—and what’s around it can include things over six miles away.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: If one gets angry, all the others around it will get angry, so silence is a rare visitor in a troop of Mankey.

Name: Primeape
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.
This attack does 50 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flavor-Text: The blood vessels in its brain are sturdier than those of other Pokémon, so it can stay healthy despite its constant raging.

Name: Onix
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Cubone
Flavor-Text: It wears its mother’s skull on its head, so no one knows what its bare face looks like. However, it’s clear that it’s always crying.

Name: Marowak
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They thump their bones rhythmically to communicate among themselves. There are nearly 50 different rhythmic patterns.

Name: Gligar
Flavor-Text: It flies straight at its target’s face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

Name: Gliscor
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey… Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

Name: Timburr
Flavor-Text: Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.

Name: Gurdurr
Flavor-Text: Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there’s skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.

Name: Conkeldurr
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.

Name: Mienfoo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: When Mienshao lets out a bizarre wail, you’re in danger. A flurry of kicks and chops too fast to see is about to be unleashed!

Name: Rolycoly
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.

Name: Carkol
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Coalossal
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: While it’s engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

Name: Silicobra
Flavor-Text: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

Name: Sandaconda
Effects: Discard the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each [F] Energy card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

Name: Falinks
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

Name: Stonjourner
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Giga Hammer.
Flavor-Text: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [F] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Impact Blow.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu VMAX
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu VMAX
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their skin is so thin that they’ll be burned if sunlight hits them. When it gets cold out, they gather together to warm one another’s bodies.

Name: Golbat
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They can suck down over 10 ounces of blood in one go. They have been known to drink so much blood that they can no longer fly.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on the blood of living people and Pokémon. If it can’t drink any blood for even a short while, it becomes weak and unable to fly.

Name: Galarian Slowbro
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Poisoned.
If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon squeezes the tongue of the Shellder biting it, the Shellder will launch a toxic liquid from the tip of its shell.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: It searches for shiny things for its boss. Murkrow’s presence is said to be unlucky, so many people detest it.

Name: Honchkrow
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Asleep.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its goons take care of most of the fighting for it. The only time it dirties its own hands is in delivering a final blow to finish off an opponent.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: They make repeated eerie howls before dawn to call attention to their pack.

Name: Houndoom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

Name: Tyranitar V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.
If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

Name: Steelix
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Bronzor
Flavor-Text: Polishing Bronzor to a shine makes its surface reflect the truth, according to common lore. Be that as it may, Bronzor hates being polished.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [M] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Slash attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [M] Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.

Name: Honedge
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Effects: Discard 2 [M] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.
Flavor-Text: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

Name: Corviknight V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Sky Hurricane.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Corviknight VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s Pokémon’s Abilities done to this Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use G-Max Hurricane.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Spearow
Flavor-Text: Due to its short wings, it can’t fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

Name: Fearow
Flavor-Text: Carrying food through Fearow’s territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

Name: Lickitung
Flavor-Text: It licks filth clean with its tongue. Whatever it licks always stinks afterward, so whether it’s really clean is…questionable.

Name: Lickilicky
Effects: Your opponent chooses to discard the top 3 cards of their deck or to discard 3 cards from their hand.
Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tongue much more skillfully than its hands or its feet. It can deftly pick up a single small bean with its tongue.

Name: Glameow
Flavor-Text: When it’s happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Draw 3 cards. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

Name: Stoutland V
Effects: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 60 more damage to your opponent’s [G] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

Name: Drampa
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
This attack does 10 damage for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

Name: Bruno
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw 7 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Camping Gear
Description: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Cheryl
Description: Heal all damage from each of your Evolution Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy from the Pokémon that were healed in this way.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Energy Recycler
Description: Shuffle up to 5 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Escape Rope
Description: Each player switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. Your opponent switches first. (If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, they don’t switch Pokémon.)
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Exp. Share
Description: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move a basic Energy from that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Fan of Waves
Description: Put a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon on the bottom of their deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Korrina’s Focus
Description: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Level Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Phoebe
Description: During this turn, damage from your Pokémon VMAX’s attacks isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rapid Strike Scroll of Swirls
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Description: The Rapid Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Rapid Strike Style Mustard
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Put a Rapid Strike Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Description: The Single Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Single Strike Style Mustard
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Search your deck for a Single Strike Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Sordward & Shielbert
Description: Choose a Trainer card from your discard pile. Then, ask your opponent if you may put it into your hand. If yes, put that card into your hand. If no, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tool Jammer
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, Pokémon Tools attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon have no effect, except for Tool Jammer.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Tower of Darkness
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may draw 2 cards. In order to use this effect, that player must discard a Single Strike card from their hand.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Tower of Waters
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Rapid Strike Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C][C] less.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Urn of Vitality
Description: Shuffle up to 2 Single Strike Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rapid Strike Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to a Rapid Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Rapid Strike Pokémon, discard this card.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of [W] Energy and [F] Energy.

Name: Single Strike Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to a Single Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Single Strike Pokémon, discard this card.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [F] and [D] Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time, and the attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Kricketune V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 4 cards in your hand instead. You can’t use more than 1 Exciting Stage Ability each turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flapple V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C][C] more.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Victini V
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Empoleon V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Basic Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Empoleon V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Basic Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes).
Effects: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Koko V
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Necrozma V
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [F] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Impact Blow.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [F] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Impact Blow.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tyranitar V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tyranitar V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Corviknight V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Sky Hurricane.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Stoutland V
Effects: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bruno
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw 7 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cheryl
Description: Heal all damage from each of your Evolution Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy from the Pokémon that were healed in this way.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Effects: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

Name: Victini VMAX
Effects: Attach up to 3 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Tapu Koko VMAX
Effects: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, their Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.

Name: Octillery
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Houndoom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.
